channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Heavy security after deadly violence in Kenya, as thousands mourn former Prime Minister Raila Odinga
item.description: NAIROBI, Kenya (AP) — Heavy security was deployed to Kenya’s western city of Kisumu before the final public viewing of the body of former Prime Minister Raila Odinga on Saturday, after five people died in the last two public funeral events.
item.dc:creator: EVELYNE MUSAMBI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Work begins to restore power to Ukraine’s Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant
item.description: Work has begun to repair the damaged power supply to Ukraine’s Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant, the head of the U.N.’s nuclear watchdog said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Palestinian death toll in Gaza tops 68,000, as Israel identifies the remains of one more hostage
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israel said the remains of another hostage that Hamas handed over the day before were identified as Eliyahu Margalit, as the Palestinian death toll surpassed 68,000 people amid searching beneath the rubble.
item.dc:creator: SAM MEDNICK and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Afghan and Pakistani delegations in Doha for crisis talks over cross-border violence
item.description: ISLAMABAD (AP) — Afghan and Pakistani delegations were in the Qatari capital, Doha, on Saturday, for talks on defusing the deadliest crisis between them in several years, after more than a week of fighting killed dozens of people and injured hundreds on both sides.
item.dc:creator: RIAZAT BUTT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: One scandal too many forces UK monarchy to sideline Prince Andrew after years of tabloid fodder
item.description: Prince Andrew has been banished from the royal whirl after emails emerged this week showing that he had remained in contact with the convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein longer than he previously admitted.
item.dc:creator: Danica Kirka, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Gaza famine claims face mounting scrutiny as mortality data falls far short of predictions
item.description: Experts challenge Gaza famine claims by the Integrated Food Security Phase Classification, citing insufficient mortality data and declining food prices.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UK sees lessons from Northern Ireland that could aid the fragile Gaza ceasefire process
item.description: Britain believes its experience in Northern Ireland could help achieve a lasting ceasefire in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: Sylvia Hui And Jill Lawless, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Deadly floods in Mexico another sign of need for improved severe weather warnings
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — The most recent torrential rains in east-central Mexico, which have left at least 72 dead and dozens more missing, have raised questions again about the government’s ability to alert people to severe weather in time.
item.dc:creator: MARÍA VERZA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: After Zelenskyy meeting, Trump calls on Ukraine and Russia to ‘stop where they are’ and end the war
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Friday called on Kyiv and Moscow to “stop where they are” and end their brutal war following a lengthy White House meeting with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, SEUNG MIN KIM, MICHELLE L. PRICE and CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Drone bombing of police building not a ‘declaration of war’ from cartels, expert says
item.description: EL PASO, Texas (Border Report) – Thursday’s brazen drone bombing of a police building in Tijuana, Mexico, likely was meant as a warning and not a declaration of war from a criminal organization, a private security expert says.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, NewsNation

item.title: Israel received coffin of missing hostage handed over to Red Cross, prime minister's office says
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israel received the remains of another hostage from Gaza on Friday, the prime minister’s office said, a handover from Hamas as the militant group worked to shore up a ceasefire by using bulldozers in a search for bodies under the rubble in the war-scarred enclave.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Prince Andrew gives up his Duke of York title as Epstein allegations refuse to fade
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Prince Andrew said Friday he is giving up his royal title of the Duke of York and other honors after his friendship with sex offender Jeffrey Epstein returned to the headlines.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS and PAN PYLAS, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: 5 takeaways from the Trump-Zelensky meeting
item.description: President Trump hosted Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky on Friday at the White House, part of a renewed push to end Russia’s more than three-year war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: After years of socialist rule, Bolivia’s presidential runoff tests how far and fast it veers right
item.description: EL ALTO, Bolivia (AP) — After nearly two decades of one-party rule, three years of an accelerating currency crisis and too many months of mind-numbing fuel lines, Bolivia is lurching to the right.
item.dc:creator: ISABEL DEBRE and CARLOS VALDEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump drops f-bomb in Zelensky meeting about Maduro
item.description: "He doesn't want to f‑‑‑ with the U.S.," Trump said, speaking from the White House during a lunch meeting with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: Orbán celebrates Hungary as 'the only place in Europe' where a Trump-Putin meeting can be held
item.description: BUDAPEST, Hungary (AP) — Hungary's nationalist Prime Minister Viktor Orbán on Friday celebrated his country's status as the host of upcoming talks between U.S. President Donald Trump and Russia's Vladimir Putin, a meeting where the two leaders are expected to discuss an end to the war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Increased US pressure on Venezuela raises specter of regime change
item.description: President Trump's authorization of covert CIA strikes in Venezuela raises concerns of regime change and potential blowback from Caracas.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: Eric Trump explains father's hot mic moment with Indonesian president
item.description: Eric Trump sought to clarify Thursday what President Trump and Indonesian President Prabowo Subianto talked about during a hot mic moment when the commander in chief's son came up during a conversation.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mancini, The Hill

item.title: King Charles III's visit to Vatican marks a historic step in path of unity between two churches
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — The Catholic Church and Church of England, divided for centuries over issues that now include the ordination of female priests, will take a historic step on the path to unity next week when Britain’s King Charles III and Pope Leo XIV pray together in the Sistine Chapel, officials
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hamas captors once held ordinary jobs
item.description: Israeli hostage Tal Shoham describes increased faith and gratitude after enduring torture and starvation during 500 days held by Hamas in Gaza tunnels.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UN condemns military takeover in Madagascar as coup leader to be quickly sworn in as president
item.description: ANTANANARIVO, Madagascar (AP) — The United Nations chief on Thursday condemned the military takeover in Madagascar as an army colonel who led the coup was set to be quickly sworn in as president just three days after announcing soldiers had taken charge. U.N.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump: 'No choice but to go in and kill' Hamas if Gaza bloodshed doesn't stop
item.description: President Trump said Thursday that if Hamas does not heed repeated warnings to stop bloodshed in Gaza, the U.S. will have “no choice” but to use lethal force. “If Hamas continues to kill people in Gaza, which was not the Deal, we will have no choice but to go in and kill them.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Trump signals new trust in Erdogan, raising concerns over Turkey’s ambitions in Gaza and beyond
item.description: Turkey seeks expanded influence in Gaza through construction contracts and peacekeeping roles as President Recep Tayyip Erdogan rebuilds trust with Washington after the ceasefire.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russian barrage causes blackouts in Ukraine as Zelenskyy seeks Trump's help
item.description: Russia battered Ukraine’s energy facilities with hundreds of drones and dozens of missiles in its latest heavy bombardment.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Florida doctor detained by Israel during aid mission to Gaza describes experience
item.description: Two Florida residents who were detained by Israel during an aid mission to Gaza are describing their experience in a Wednesday press conference.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says Modi told him India would stop buying Russian oil
item.description: President Trump said Wednesday that Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi told him that India would stop buying Russian oil, a potentially significant move to further pressure Moscow over its war in Ukraine. "I was not happy that India was buying oil.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Russia releases JFK files to Rep. Anna Paulina Luna
item.description: The transfer of the 350-page document was confirmed this week by Russia’s ambassador to the U.S. and Luna, who chairs a House panel on government secrets.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Kate Middleton and Prince William set to move into 'forever home': Report
item.description: The Prince and Princess of Wales, along with their children, have scheduled early November for when they plan to settle into their new Windsor residence.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: Trump confirms CIA authorization for operations in Venezuela
item.description: It is unclear whether any operations are planned or if the authorization serves as a contingency measure.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Former Kenyan premier Raila Odinga, a key figure in African democracy efforts, dies at 80
item.description: NAIROBI, Kenya (AP) — Raila Odinga, a former prime minister of Kenya and perennial presidential candidate whose populist campaigns challenged one-party rule, rattled authorities and gave him outsized influence on political life in the East African country, died Wednesday of a heart attack while trav
item.dc:creator: EVELYNE MUSAMBI and RODNEY MUHUMUZA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Remains released by Hamas not a hostage, Israel's military says
item.description: The original framework of a U.S.-brokered ceasefire required Hamas to return all 28 deceased hostages Monday.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: Trump’s swipe at ‘weak’ leaders highlights the shifting global influence of Western nations
item.description: Amid Trump’s visit to Egypt and Israel to celebrate the release of hostages held by Hamas and to usher in a new era of Mideast peace, some say Western influence is waning.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A new form of tourism: US residents seek tattoos in Mexico
item.description: JUAREZ, Mexico (Border Report) – U.S. residents often cite lower prices and personalized attention when asked why they travel to see doctors and dentists in Mexico. The same holds true for tattoo artists.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, NewsNation

item.title: Snipers on stadium roof amid heavy security for Italy's win over Israel in World Cup qualifying
item.description: UDINE, Italy (AP) — The World Cup qualifier between Italy and Israel took place amid a heavy police presence that included snipers on the stadium roof.
item.dc:creator: DANIELLA MATAR, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Freed hostages' loved ones thank Trump for reuniting them after more than two years
item.description: Several families of the freed Israeli hostages thanked President Donald Trump and his team for brokering the Israel-Hamas deal that brought them home.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hamas holds public executions, tightens grip on Gaza
item.description: CAIRO/JERUSALEM, Oct 14 (Reuters) - Israel restricted aid into Gaza and kept the enclave's border shut on Tuesday while re-emergent Hamas fighters demonstrated their grip by executing men in the street, darkening the outlook for U.S. President Donald Trump's plan to end the war.
item.dc:creator: Nidal al-Mughrabi and Maayan Lubell, NewsNation

item.title: After hostages and prisoners freed, complex issues remain for Israel-Hamas
item.description: Issues such as whether Hamas will disarm and who will govern Gaza — and the question of Palestinian statehood — remain unresolved, highlighting the fragility of an agreement that for now only pauses the deadliest conflict in the history of Israel and the Palestinians.
item.dc:creator: Wafaa Shurafa, WFLA, NBC
